findings.evidence: Grep hits show public upstream-style names in the prelude: lines 38, 52, 71, 137, 196, 295, 310; the file docstring says these are declarations from pending Mathlib universal-cover work.
findings.fix: Keep the vendored material in a TauCeti-specific namespace/prefix, or add a clear bump strategy that removes/replaces the shim before importing a Mathlib version containing these names.
findings.issue: Public vendored declarations are added under root/Mathlib namespaces (`exists_monotone_partition_Icc`, `Path.subpathOn`, `Path.Homotopic.of_trans_symm`, etc.), creating a Mathlib-bump compatibility break when the upstream PRs land.

summary: The PR adds new API rather than renaming existing TauCeti declarations, but the vendored prelude exposes upstream-style names directly in public Mathlib namespaces, so a Mathlib bump that lands any of those declarations will fail with duplicate declarations.
